+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-112
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-23_10-52-47+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-110_EXEC_2026-05-23_10-32-36+0300.md — read-only audit (no edits)
+- tools/architecture-lints/src/kernel_isolation.rs — read-only audit (no edits)
+- tools/architecture-lints/tests/kernel_isolation_test.rs — read-only audit (no edits)
+- Cargo.toml — read-only audit (no edits)
+- crates/io-obj/Cargo.toml — read-only audit (no edits)
+- crates/io-obj/src/lib.rs — read-only audit (no edits)
+- crates/io-audio/Cargo.toml — read-only audit (no edits)
+- crates/io-audio/src/lib.rs — read-only audit (no edits)
+- crates/io-3mf/Cargo.toml — read-only audit (no edits)
+- crates/io-3mf/src/lib.rs — read-only audit (no edits)
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-112_TASK_2026-05-23_10-45-49+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. This dispatch is a read-only remaining-IO-format metadata
+  preflight audit; no source, test, manifest, lockfile, lint, workflow,
+  script, doc, schema, or existing packet was modified.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added or modified.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None added or modified. The only new files written by this dispatch
+  are this `ISSUE-112_EXEC_*.md` packet (and its optional `.meta.json`
+  sidecar produced if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` is later invoked by
+  the orchestrator). Both are protocol artifacts explicitly permitted
+  by the Task Packet's `MAY add new files` clause.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Skip — bullet lists above are sufficient.
+
+## Remaining IO Formats Metadata Preflight Answers
+
+### 1. What did task #13 find, and what remains for this dispatch?
+
+Task #13 / `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-110_EXEC_2026-05-23_10-32-36+0300.md`
+established the `[package.metadata.rge] formats = [...]` metadata shape
+that the `kernel-isolation` lint reads, enumerated all seven workspace
+IO members (`io-gltf`, `io-step`, `io-stl`, `io-obj`, `io-image`,
+`io-audio`, `io-3mf` — root `Cargo.toml` lines 96-103, the "Tier 2 —
+Importers / exporters" block), and scoped the first four exact format
+arrays:
+
+- `rge-io-gltf`: `["gltf", "glb"]`
+- `rge-io-image`: `["png", "jpg", "jpeg", "exr", "hdr"]`
+- `rge-io-step`: `["step", "stp", "iges", "igs"]`
+- `rge-io-stl`: `["stl"]`
+
+It deliberately deferred ownership decisions for the remaining three
+crates (`io-obj`, `io-audio`, `io-3mf`) and proposed only a
+four-crate, manifest-only follow-up. What remains for this dispatch is
+to:
+
+1. Confirm the remaining three exact format arrays for `rge-io-obj`,
+   `rge-io-audio`, and `rge-io-3mf` from the inspected evidence.
+2. Resolve the named aliases (`obj`/`mtl`, `wav`/`ogg`/`oga`, `flac`,
+   `mp3`/`mpeg`, `3mf`) so that no ownership ambiguity remains across
+   the seven IO crates.
+3. Scope one later combined **seven-crate, manifest-only** follow-up
+   that adds `[package.metadata.rge] formats = [...]` to exactly the
+   seven IO manifests and nothing else.
+
+### 2. How does the lint detect IO crates and read metadata?
+
+Evidence from `tools/architecture-lints/src/kernel_isolation.rs` (read
+in full, 161 lines) and `tools/architecture-lints/tests/kernel_isolation_test.rs`
+(read in full, 393 lines):
+
+- **IO crate detection** — `is_io_crate` (source lines 54-71) flags a
+  workspace package as an IO crate via two OR-ed checks:
+  1. Package name starts with `rge-io-` (canonical workspace prefix;
+     e.g. `rge-io-gltf`) or with bare `io-` (synthetic fixture
+     convention used by the integration tests).
+  2. Manifest-path fallback: when the manifest lives at
+     `crates/io-*/Cargo.toml` (relative to workspace root), the crate
+     is detected even if the package name does not match.
+  Test 6 (`test_rge_prefixed_io_crates_overlap_detected_via_name_path`,
+  test lines 257-299) exercises the name-prefix path with directories
+  outside `crates/io-*`; test 8
+  (`test_manifest_path_fallback_alone_detects_io_crate`, test lines
+  347-392) exercises the manifest-path fallback with package names that
+  do not start with `io-` or `rge-io-`.
+- **Metadata opt-in shape** — each IO crate opts in by adding to its
+  own `Cargo.toml`:
+  ```toml
+  [package.metadata.rge]
+  formats = ["gltf", "glb"]
+  ```
+  (source doc-comment lines 19-26, mirrored verbatim by
+  `pkg_toml_with_formats` in the integration tests, test lines 47-58).
+- **String shape** — strings are extension names: lower-case, no
+  leading dot (source doc-comment line 28). `extract_formats` (source
+  lines 77-87) walks
+  `pkg.metadata.get("rge").and_then(|rge| rge.get("formats")).and_then(|f| f.as_array())`
+  and calls `str::to_lowercase` on every entry (line 84). No alias
+  normalization is performed: `jpg` and `jpeg` are treated as distinct
+  literals; the lint only compares lower-cased literal strings.
+- **Overlap = violation** — a format extension claimed by ≥ 2 detected
+  IO crates is a violation; the lint exits 1 (source lines 128-157;
+  tests 4, 5, 6, 8). Duplicate literal strings across IO crates are
+  therefore violations regardless of whether they refer to the same
+  underlying format family.
+- **Missing metadata = stderr warning, not violation** — Option B,
+  source doc-comment lines 30-35 and the `None =>` branch at lines
+  109-115 print
+  `warning: io-* crate `<name>` missing `package.metadata.rge.formats` declaration`
+  to stderr and continue; the lint still exits 0 if no overlaps exist.
+  This is pinned by test 7
+  (`test_missing_metadata_is_warning_not_violation`, test lines
+  301-334).
+
+### 3. What exact remaining three-crate format arrays are implied?
+
+Based exclusively on the inspected manifests and crate-level
+doc-comments for the three remaining IO crates:
+
+- `rge-io-obj`: `["obj", "mtl"]`
+- `rge-io-audio`: `["wav", "ogg", "oga", "flac", "mp3", "mpeg"]`
+- `rge-io-3mf`: `["3mf"]`
+
+Evidence per crate:
+
+- **`rge-io-obj`** — `crates/io-obj/Cargo.toml` line 3 description
+  "OBJ import/export". `crates/io-obj/src/lib.rs` is a stub
+  ("Architecture frozen at v0.8; implementation pending"). OBJ
+  geometry files carry the `.obj` extension; their companion material
+  library carries the `.mtl` extension and is referenced from the OBJ
+  via the `mtllib` statement (Wavefront OBJ specification). Both
+  extensions belong to the OBJ format family and to no other workspace
+  IO crate.
+- **`rge-io-audio`** — `crates/io-audio/Cargo.toml` line 3 description
+  "WAV/OGG/FLAC/MP3 via Kira". `crates/io-audio/src/lib.rs` is a
+  stub. The four-format manifest description maps to the canonical
+  audio container extensions: WAV → `.wav`; OGG → `.ogg` plus the
+  audio-only `.oga` variant (RFC 5334 §10); FLAC → `.flac`; MP3 →
+  `.mp3` plus the MPEG-1/2 Audio Layer III container `.mpeg`
+  occasionally used on disk for the same payload. Because the lint
+  string-compares lower-cased literals with no alias collapse, every
+  on-disk spelling that belongs to this crate must be listed
+  individually.
+- **`rge-io-3mf`** — `crates/io-3mf/Cargo.toml` line 3 description
+  "3MF import/export". `crates/io-3mf/src/lib.rs` is a stub. 3MF
+  files carry exactly one canonical extension `.3mf`; lower-cased and
+  without a leading dot the array entry is the string `"3mf"`.
+
+### 4. Which ownership ambiguities and aliases are resolved, and which remain?
+
+Resolved (the implied three arrays above already encode these, and
+combined with the four task #13 arrays leave no overlap with any
+other IO crate):
+
+- **`obj` and `mtl`** — both belong to `rge-io-obj`. No other IO
+  workspace member (`io-gltf`, `io-image`, `io-step`, `io-stl`,
+  `io-audio`, `io-3mf`) claims either string in its array, and the
+  task #13 four-crate arrays do not contain `obj` or `mtl`. The lint
+  performs no alias collapse, so both spellings are listed under
+  `rge-io-obj` and only there.
+- **`wav`** — belongs to `rge-io-audio`. The crate manifest names
+  WAV as the first format; no other IO crate claims `wav`.
+- **`ogg` and `oga`** — both belong to `rge-io-audio`. `ogg` is the
+  general Ogg container extension; `oga` is the audio-only Ogg
+  variant defined in RFC 5334 §10. Because the lint compares
+  lower-cased literals only and performs no alias collapse (see §2),
+  both spellings must be listed under `rge-io-audio` if its ownership
+  of the OGG family is accepted. No other IO crate claims either
+  string.
+- **`flac`** — belongs to `rge-io-audio`. Single canonical extension;
+  no other IO crate claims `flac`.
+- **`mp3` and `mpeg`** — both belong to `rge-io-audio`. `mp3` is the
+  canonical MPEG-1/2 Audio Layer III extension; `mpeg` is the
+  occasionally-used disk-side spelling for the same audio payload.
+  Same lint constraint as for OGG: both literals must be listed
+  individually under `rge-io-audio` because the lint performs no
+  alias collapse. No other IO crate claims either string.
+- **`3mf`** — belongs to `rge-io-3mf`, listed exactly as the
+  lower-case, no-leading-dot extension string `"3mf"`. No other IO
+  crate claims `3mf`.
+
+No ownership ambiguity remains among the seven IO crates after
+combining the task #13 arrays
+(`["gltf","glb"]` / `["png","jpg","jpeg","exr","hdr"]` /
+`["step","stp","iges","igs"]` / `["stl"]`) with the three remaining
+arrays above. Cross-checked: the union of all seven arrays is
+`{gltf, glb, png, jpg, jpeg, exr, hdr, step, stp, iges, igs, stl,
+obj, mtl, wav, ogg, oga, flac, mp3, mpeg, 3mf}` — twenty-one distinct
+lower-cased extension strings with no duplicates. The lint compares
+these literals only, so this set guarantees no overlap violation.
+
+No alias remains unresolved; this dispatch therefore does not halt on
+ownership.
+
+### 5. What is the smallest safe seven-crate manifest follow-up?
+
+A single later **manifest-only** dispatch that adds exactly one
+`[package.metadata.rge]` block per IO crate to exactly these seven
+manifests and no others:
+
+```toml
+# crates/io-gltf/Cargo.toml
+[package.metadata.rge]
+formats = ["gltf", "glb"]
+
+# crates/io-image/Cargo.toml
+[package.metadata.rge]
+formats = ["png", "jpg", "jpeg", "exr", "hdr"]
+
+# crates/io-step/Cargo.toml
+[package.metadata.rge]
+formats = ["step", "stp", "iges", "igs"]
+
+# crates/io-stl/Cargo.toml
+[package.metadata.rge]
+formats = ["stl"]
+
+# crates/io-obj/Cargo.toml
+[package.metadata.rge]
+formats = ["obj", "mtl"]
+
+# crates/io-audio/Cargo.toml
+[package.metadata.rge]
+formats = ["wav", "ogg", "oga", "flac", "mp3", "mpeg"]
+
+# crates/io-3mf/Cargo.toml
+[package.metadata.rge]
+formats = ["3mf"]
+```
+
+That seven-crate follow-up MUST be scoped manifest-only and MUST
+explicitly forbid all of:
+
+- **Source changes** — no edits under `crates/io-*/src/**` or any
+  other crate's source tree.
+- **Test changes** — no edits under `crates/io-*/tests/**`,
+  `tools/architecture-lints/tests/**`, or any other test tree.
+- **Lint behavior changes** — no edits to
+  `tools/architecture-lints/src/kernel_isolation.rs` or any other
+  file under `tools/architecture-lints/src/**`. In particular, no
+  alias-normalization logic is added to the lint; alias coverage
+  stays a manifest concern because the lint reads literals without
+  normalization (§2 / §4).
+- **Workflow changes** — no edits under `.github/workflows/**`.
+- **Script changes** — no edits under `scripts/**`, `.ai/**`, or any
+  PowerShell/shell tooling.
+- **Docs changes** — no edits under `docs/**` or to top-level
+  documentation (`README.md`, `Status.md`, `change.md`, `HANDOFF.md`,
+  etc.).
+- **`Cargo.lock` changes** — the follow-up adds manifest metadata
+  only; if `cargo metadata` regenerates `Cargo.lock` as a side
+  effect, that lockfile drift must be reverted before commit so the
+  follow-up's diff is exactly the seven `[package.metadata.rge]`
+  blocks.
+- **Alias-normalization changes** — no rewrite of any array to
+  collapse `jpg`/`jpeg`, `step`/`stp`, `iges`/`igs`, `ogg`/`oga`, or
+  `mp3`/`mpeg`. Each extension spelling stays a distinct literal
+  string in the manifest array, because the lint compares literals
+  only.
+
+The follow-up MUST NOT broaden scope beyond the seven IO crate
+manifests named above (no other crate gains a `[package.metadata.rge]`
+block in the same dispatch). The follow-up MUST NOT be split into
+multiple manifest tasks; the seven blocks land together so the
+kernel-isolation lint transitions from seven stderr warnings to seven
+fully-declared IO crates in a single atomic change.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- **Read-only inspection** using `Read`, `Glob`, and `Grep` only,
+  covering every required path listed in `Acceptance Criteria`:
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-110_EXEC_2026-05-23_10-32-36+0300.md` — read in
+    full (329 lines). PASS.
+  - `tools/architecture-lints/src/kernel_isolation.rs` — read in full
+    (161 lines). PASS.
+  - `tools/architecture-lints/tests/kernel_isolation_test.rs` — read
+    in full (393 lines). PASS.
+  - root `Cargo.toml` — workspace IO member block at lines 96-103 read
+    and confirmed (the seven "Tier 2 — Importers / exporters"
+    members). PASS.
+  - `crates/io-obj/**` — `Cargo.toml` (16 lines) and `src/lib.rs` (5
+    lines) read; full file listing enumerated via Glob (exactly two
+    files). PASS.
+  - `crates/io-audio/**` — `Cargo.toml` (16 lines) and `src/lib.rs` (5
+    lines) read; full file listing enumerated via Glob (exactly two
+    files). PASS.
+  - `crates/io-3mf/**` — `Cargo.toml` (16 lines) and `src/lib.rs` (5
+    lines) read; full file listing enumerated via Glob (exactly two
+    files). PASS.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` BEFORE writing this packet:
+  ```
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-112_TASK_2026-05-23_10-45-49+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-112_TASK_2026-05-23_10-45-49+0300.meta.json
+  ```
+  Only the planner-attributed TASK packet and its sidecar — no
+  executor-attributed source/manifest/lockfile/lint/workflow/script/
+  test/doc/`.ai` changes. PASS.

+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- Confirm that `oga` (audio-only Ogg variant, RFC 5334 §10) belongs in
+  `rge-io-audio`'s array alongside `ogg`. The `kira` audio backend
+  referenced in the manifest description does not document `oga`
+  separately, but the lint's literal-string semantics require both
+  spellings to be claimed if the OGG container is owned here.
+  Otherwise a future `.oga` importer added to a different crate would
+  not trigger the overlap violation — the very gap the lint exists to
+  catch. The Task Packet explicitly enumerates `oga` in question 4 as
+  ownership to resolve, so this dispatch resolves it under
+  `rge-io-audio`.
+- Confirm that `mpeg` belongs in `rge-io-audio`'s array alongside
+  `mp3`. The crate description names "MP3"; some pipelines also use
+  the `.mpeg` extension for MPEG-1/2 Audio Layer III audio payloads,
+  and the lint compares literals only. The Task Packet explicitly
+  enumerates `mpeg` in question 4 as ownership to resolve, so this
+  dispatch resolves it under `rge-io-audio`. If the reviewer prefers
+  to scope `.mpeg` ownership to a hypothetical future video crate
+  instead, that decision must be recorded before the seven-crate
+  manifest follow-up lands so the `rge-io-audio` array is narrowed
+  accordingly.
+- Confirm that `mtl` belongs in `rge-io-obj`'s array alongside `obj`.
+  The crate description names "OBJ" only; the `.mtl` material library
+  is referenced from `.obj` via `mtllib` per the Wavefront OBJ
+  specification, so both extensions belong to the same crate family.
+  The Task Packet explicitly enumerates `mtl` in question 4 as
+  ownership to resolve, so this dispatch resolves it under
+  `rge-io-obj`.
